design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed levels as Bonkers the cartoon character, progressing through increasingly complex platforming challenges with environmental hazards and enemy encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision platforming execution against level design complexity; collision-based hazard avoidance in themed environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a wacky cartoon character (Bonkers) navigating surreal, Disney-inspired Hollywood-themed worlds with slapstick physics and chaotic level design
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on pattern memorization and precise execution; unforgiving checkpoint placement suggests developer commitment to arcade-style difficulty philosophy rather than modern accessibility. Debug features reveal developer intent for extensive playtesting but final release maintains punishing design.

difficulty.lives_system: Traditional lives-based system typical of early 90s platformers; player must memorize level layouts and enemy patterns to progress
difficulty.continues_system: Implied continue system through level progression mechanics, though exact parameters not documented
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system present but appears spaced to enforce pattern memorization and player memorization of hazard sequences
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; no documented difficulty settings or assist modes. Game presents fixed challenge curve. However, hidden level select suggests speedrunner-friendly design philosophy

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression based on stage completion; hidden level select (accessible via cheat codes in debug build) indicates sequential unlocking of themed world stages
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select encourages speedrunning and practice routing
Debug collision toggle and infinite jump features suggest developer anticipated ROM hacking community engagement
Themed level variety (implied by 'Hollywood Daisakusen' Japanese title) encourages exploring different environmental challenges

world_building_techniques: Licensed Disney character (Bonkers) provides thematic consistency
Themed level environments (Hollywood context) create visual cohesion without explicit narrative delivery
Japanese subtitle 'Hollywood Daisakusen' (Hollywood Great Strategy) suggests adventure framing but not implemented through cutscenes or dialogue

danger_signals: Game marked 'To investigate' on TCRF suggests incomplete documentation of core mechanics and design intent
Debug flag Bit 08 explicitly marked 'Unknown' - developer intent partially obscured
Sparse source material indicates limited community analysis compared to other SNES platformers
environmental_storytelling: Hollywood-themed level design creates implicit narrative context without explicit storytelling
Character-themed platforming (Bonkers is cartoon character) establishes genre expectation: whimsical chaos platforming

sources_used: tcrf - Bonkers (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor (debug flags, level select, development information)
wikipedia - Bonkers disambiguation page (confirmed SNES game existence and Japanese title)
